channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Southeast Asian Leaders Tackle Iran War Vulnerabilities
item.description: Global market shocks spark calls for a regional power grid and emergency fuel stockpile.

item.title: What in the World?
item.description: Test yourself on the week of May 2: The U.S. announces a drawdown in Germany, Taiwan’s president concludes a foreign visit, and Sudan lobs accusations at Ethiopia.

item.title: A Foreign Correspondent of Animal Kingdoms
item.description: How a headstrong field biologist helped birth the worldwide conservation movement.

item.title: Guatemala, once Latin America’s rule-of-law beacon, has new hope
item.description: But a new attorney-general may not be enough to revive the fight against corruption

item.title: How to Choose a Gift for Trump
item.description: An expert in diplomatic gift-giving describes the use and abuse of official presents.

item.title: A Confused ‘Animal Farm’ for a Confused Time
item.description: What the latest Orwell adaptation says about our politics.

item.title: Trump’s Tariffs Just Got Struck Down in Court—Again
item.description: Courts keep taking a skeptical view of the executive branch’s overreach.

item.title: Do house plants improve air quality?
item.description: Air purifiers are more effective and easier to maintain

item.title: Do houseplants improve air quality?
item.description: Air purifiers are more effective and easier to maintain

item.title: Cover Story newsletter: The summit of suspicion
item.description: How we chose this week’s image

item.title: The Surprisingly Normal Streets of Tehran
item.description: U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran have led regular people to rally around the flag—for now.

item.title: Europe Is Losing the Energy-Security Battle to China
item.description: While tensions around the Strait of Hormuz are rattling global oil markets, energy security is no longer defined solely by access to fossil fuels. What China has understood, and Europe has not, is that security now depends on electricity systems that can deliver low-cost power at scale and support heavy industry.

item.title: In a Single Day, 3 Democracies Pushed Back Against Chinese Transnational Repression
item.description: That three liberal democracies acted against Beijing’s long arm on a single day was not coordinated – but perhaps it should have been.

item.title: Europe Needs a Civic Revolution
item.description: The question for Europe is no longer whether it should become a global power, but whether it can do so democratically and in time to shape, rather than endure, the new world order that is now emerging. Fortunately, European citizens are increasingly aware of what is at stake.

item.title: The US and China Don’t Need Another Dialogue. They Need a Circuit Breaker.
item.description: A proposed Board of Trade will matter only if it can keep ordinary commercial disputes from becoming tests of national resolve.

item.title: Chinese Fighter Sales Surged After the 2025 India-Pakistan Aerial Clashes
item.description: The India-Pakistan clashes gave the J-10C a new, highly coveted status: battled-tested.

item.title: The Achilles’ Heel of China’s Supply Chain Strategy: New Technology
item.description: The NEV industry is China’s best – maybe only – hope for leading a major tech manufacturing sector. But new technologies might erase China’s advantage.

item.title: A Latin American Alternative to Bukele?
item.description: Though few have noticed, Mexico has reduced its daily homicide count by 41% over the past 18 months, owing to President Claudia Sheinbaum’s pioneering new strategy for confronting organized crime. Such a sharp reduction is an historic achievement for Mexico – and puts Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele's thuggish methods to shame.

item.title: Can Pakistan Make Its Space Program Great Again?
item.description: None of Pakistan’s recent space achievements would be possible without Chinese help. Is Pakistan building a space program or just leasing one?

item.title: Europe’s Left and China: Beyond Campism and Atlanticism
item.description: The European left can make Europe’s China policy more socially intelligent. But leftists must not forget that Chinese society is more than the Chinese state.

item.title: Is the Global Financial System Making the Hormuz Shock Worse?
item.description: The existing global financial architecture was supposed to ensure stability by providing liquidity when capital retreats and cushioning vulnerable economies against shocks. So, why is the system doing exactly the opposite?

item.title: 8+1: The New Geometry of Mongolian Foreign Policy
item.description: With President Khurelsukh’s state visit to Kazakhstan, and his appearance at the Regional Ecological Summit in Astana, Mongolia has reached both shores of the Caspian.

item.title: India’s Bangladesh Policy Quagmire: Big Brother’s Recalcitrance and the Chinese Embrace
item.description: The BJP, or its allies, are now in power in all five Indian states that share a border with Bangladesh.

item.title: China Is Transforming Brazil’s Car Market
item.description: BYD’s landmark sales fuel debate over working conditions and the country’s relationship with China.

item.title: Pakistan’s Rising Role in West Asia’s Shifting Geopolitical Landscape
item.description: Irrespective of the outcomes of the U.S.-Iran talks, Pakistan’s expanded role in West Asia as a security stabilizer and an assertive diplomatic actor is here to stay.

item.title: The GPM and Japan’s First Step into Party Diplomacy
item.description: When politics falls behind the economy.

item.title: Takaichi Sanae’s Canberra Visit
item.description: A new era of ‘like-minded partnerships’

item.title: ASEAN Unity and Japan’s Role Amid US-China Tensions
The Missing Linchpin of European Hard Power
item.description: Spurred by the Ukraine war and deepening doubts about America’s commitment to its NATO allies, Europe has begun building the financial and industrial foundations of military power. Unless it also establishes a clear political authority capable of directing that power, however, Europe could end up more vulnerable than ever.

item.title: The Revolution in Bago: A Crucial Battle for Myanmar’s Future
item.description: Even as the military attempts to rebrand itself as a civilian government, resistance groups have established a foothold in the crucial central region.

item.title: Is Singapore’s Legal System Becoming More Draconian?
item.description: A series of recent cases raises the question of whether such punitive measures are necessary for maintaining the country's clean and orderly reputation.

item.title: Thai, Cambodian PMs Agree to ‘Confidence Building’ Measures on Border
item.description: Relations between Bangkok and Phnom Penh remain severely strained following outbreaks of armed conflict in July and December of last year.

item.title: Why China Waits
item.description: Beijing is playing a long game on Taiwan.

item.title: The Gulf Is Still America’s to Lose
item.description: How U.S. partnerships can survive the war in Iran.

item.title: The Gulf Still Needs America
item.description: How U.S. partnerships can survive the war in Iran.

item.title: America and the Gulf Still Need Each Other
item.description: How U.S. partnerships can survive the war in Iran.

item.title: How Corruption Exacerbates the Climate Crisis in the Philippines
item.description: The pursuit of bold climate solutions cannot be divorced from the goals of fighting graft and ending elite impunity.

item.title: As Summit Gets Underway, ASEAN Calls For Joint Response to Iran War Fallout
item.description: Home to around 700 million people, Southeast Asia has been one of the regions most affected by the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Secret document reveals Russia’s plans to aid Iran
item.description: Unjammable drones would be used against American forces, according to proposals seen by The Economist

item.title: China’s submarines are testing America’s undersea dominance
item.description: And naval drones will soon trouble both countries

item.title: America’s submarine dominance is under threat
item.description: Naval mockery of China is turning to alarm
